calendar.matters:
ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jul-08-2025 Off Calendar

Petition For An Order To Show Cause Why The Administrator And The Depository Should Not Be Punished For Contempt For Disobedience Of The Court?S Order For Final Distribution And Or Order Authorizing Withdrawal Of Funds From Blocked Account
Off Calendar Jul-18-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Steven Schroeder for Inheritance Funding Company, Inc., Petitioner (via Zoom).
Randall Stanton for Wells Fargo Bank, Respondent (via Zoom).

The Court noted that no appearance was made by Dave Toler. Mr. Schroeder confirmed that personal service on Mr. Toler had not been effectuated. As a result, the Court took the Orders to Show Cause off calendar.

The Court then heard argument from counsel on the issue of whether Wells Fargo acted in willful violation of a court order concerning funds held in a blocked estate account.

Mr. Schroeder argued that Wells Fargo improperly reissued a cashier's check to Inheritance Funding Company LLC at the request of Dave Toler, after the original check issued to Inheritance Funding Company, Inc. had not been used for its intended purpose. He contended that Wells Fargo failed to consider that the funds originated from a court-ordered blocked account and that the bank should have investigated the order before reissuing the funds. Mr. Schroeder maintained that this conduct undermined the purpose of court-ordered blocked accounts and constituted willful disobedience.

Mr. Stanton responded that Wells Fargo employees involved in the reissuance were unaware of the blocked account order and acted according to internal policies upon presentation of the original cashier's check and instructions from the remitter, Dave Toler. He argued that while unfortunate, the facts demonstrated negligence rather than contempt, and noted that supporting declarations were filed showing lack of knowledge of the court's order. He asserted that Mr. Toler's apparent fraudulent conduct was the root of the problem.

The Court noted the lack of case law cited supporting a finding of contempt under these specific circumstances.

Following further argument from Mr. Schroeder, the Court took the petition under submission and stated it would further consider the arguments presented.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-08-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar May-20-2025 Continued To Jul-08-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-20-2025 Continued To Jul-08-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Steven Schroeder for Inheritance Funding Company, Inc., Petitioner (via Zoom).
Randall Stanton for Wells Fargo Bank, Respondent (via Zoom).
Jeffrey Coleman, specially appearing for attorney Judy Carver, for Probate Advance, LLC, Assignee (via Zoom).

The Court asked Mr. Schroeder about proceeding with a contempt proceeding versus a civil action. Mr. Schroeder argued that Mr. Toller opened an LLC. two months after a court order, that he had checks payable to an LLC. Mr. Stanton stated that Wells Fargo complied with the Court's original order by issuing a cashier's check as directed.

After discussions, the Court ordered a briefing schedule for the legal authority regarding the amount of the cashed check. Brief to be filed by June 17, 2025. Response to be filed by June 24, 2025. All matters are continued to July 8, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Thompson. Not reported.

Petition For An Order To Show Cause Why The Administrator And The Depository Should Not Be Punished For Contempt For Disobedience Of The Court?S Order For Final Distribution And Or Order Authorizing Withdrawal Of Funds From Blocked Account
Off Calendar May-20-2025 Continued To Jul-08-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-20-2025 Continued To May-20-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-18-2025 Off Calendar

Petition For An Order To Show Cause Why The Administrator And The Depository Should Not Be Punished For Contempt For Disobedience Of The Court?S Order For Final Distribution And Or Order Authorizing Withdrawal Of Funds From Blocked Account
Off Calendar Mar-20-2025 Continued To May-20-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Steven Schroeder for Inheritance Funding Company, Inc., Petitioner (via Zoom).
Randall Stanton for Wells Fargo Bank, Respondent (via Zoom).
Jeffrey Coleman, specially appearing for attorney Judy Carver, for Probate Advance, LLC, Assignee (via Zoom).

The Court addressed the Order to Show Cause regarding Wells Fargo Bank. Based on the response filed by Mr. Stanton, the Court discharged and removed the Order to Show Cause from the calendar.

The Court asked Mr. Schroeder about proceeding with a contempt proceeding versus a civil action. Mr. Schroeder argued that Wells Fargo was in contempt under CCP 1209, et seq. Mr. Stanton asserted that Wells Fargo complied with the Court's original order by issuing a cashier's check as directed, and any subsequent misuse or fraud by Dave Toler was beyond the bank's control.

The Court explained the procedural complexities and strict requirements involved in pursuing contempt proceedings and discussed the alternative option to pursue civil remedies.

After discussions, the court took the petition under submission for further review of the relevant statutes and the parties' submissions.

Mr. Coleman informed the court of a similar situation involving Probate Advance, LLC and requested a status hearing. The Court indicated it may address this in its forthcoming order rather than setting a hearing date at this time.

The court further indicated that the Order to Show Cause issued for Dave Tolar remains on calendar and will also be address by further order.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-21-2025 Continued To Mar-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Jan-23-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Steven Schroeder for Inheritance Funding Company, Inc., Petitioner/Assignee (via Zoom).
Melissa Hoffman for Dave Toler, Respondent (via Zoom).
Todd Robie, specially appearing counsel for Judy Carver, for Probate Advance LLC, Assignee (via Zoom).

Mr. Robie reported that he was specially appearing for attorney Judy Carver, indicating that she was currently ill and requested a continuance of all matters. Ms. Hoffman requested that her Motion to be Relieved as Counsel for Dave Toler be ruled on today.

Mr. Schroeder raised concerns about unresolved issues, including improper disbursement of checks. Ms. Hoffman confirmed that contact with Mr. Toler had been lost since July or August of 2024 and stated that mail sent to his last known address in Texas was returned as undeliverable. The court found good cause to relieve Ms. Hoffman as counsel for Dave Toler and took her Motion to be Relieved as Counsel under submission with intent to grant.

The court continued the Petition for Order to Show Cause, as well as the Order to Show Cause issued by the court, to March 18, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

The court noted that Randall Stanton, attorney for Wells Fargo Bank, was not present. The court will issue an Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause to Randall Stanton to appear on March 18, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to explain why $250 in sanctions shall not be imposed for their failure to appear at today's hearing.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For An Order To Show Cause Why The Administrator And The Depository Should Not Be Punished For Contempt For Disobedience Of The Court?S Order For Final Distribution And Or Order Authorizing Withdrawal Of Funds From Blocked Account
Off Calendar Jan-21-2025 Continued To Mar-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For An Order To Show Cause Why The Administrator And The Depository Should Not Be Punished For Contempt For Disobedience Of The Court?S Order For Final Distribution And Or Order Authorizing Withdrawal Of Funds From Blocked Account
Off Calendar Dec-24-2024 Continued To Jan-21-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Steven Schroeder for Inheritance Funding Company, Inc., Petitioner/Assignee of Dave Toler (via Zoom).
Melissa Hoffman for Dave Toler, Respondent (via Zoom).
Randall W. Stanton for Wells Fargo Bank, Respondent (via Zoom).

The court noted a recently filed opposition from Wells Fargo Bank. Ms. Hoffman reported that she had lost contact with her client, Dave Toler, since approximately August 2024, and attempts to communicate with him through various means have been unsuccessful. Ms. Hoffman has filed a Motion to be Relieved as Counsel which is currently set for hearing on January 21, 2025.

Mr. Stanton explained that the bank issued checks per court order, but that Mr. Toler later had the check reissued in his name under unclear circumstances. Mr. Schroeder raised concerns about possible fraud by Mr. Toler and/or negligence on Wells Fargo's part. It was noted that $112,200 was deposited into a Northbrook Banking Trust account, raising additional questions about compliance with court orders and disbursement of funds.

The court will issue an Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause for Dave Toler to appear on January 21, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to explain why he should not be held in contempt for failing to pay Inheritance Funding Company as required by court order. The petition is also continued to be heard on the same date.

Mr. Schroeder shall file a response to Wells Fargo Bank's opposition by January 14, 2025.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

The Third Amended Report of Administrator, etc. is under submission with the court's intent to grant, but it will take a second look at the extraordinary fees.

Petition To Require Posting Of Bond, Or In The Alternative To Deposit Funds Into A Blocked Account
Off Calendar Apr-18-2023 Continued To Jun-27-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Judy Carver appeared by zoom for Probate Advance, LLC (petitioner).
Melissa Hoffman appeared by zoom for David Toler (administrator).

Mrs. Carver stated that she believes they have reached an agreement to have the proceeds deposited into a blocked account, and stated that the funds are to be wired from escrow to the blocked account. Mrs. Hoffman stated that they do not know the amount yet because the house has not been sold yet, that they hope to sell it within the next month or two, and that it is listed at about $750,000.00. The court stated that it will need proof of the blocked account. Mrs. Carver stated that she will speak with Mrs. Hoffman to see if there are any amounts that need to be kept outside of the blocked account to pay for costs. Discussion was held regarding the need to have more than one financial institution to ensure that the accounts are FDIC insured. The Petition to Require Posting of Bond or in the Alternative to Deposit Funds into a Blocked Account is continued to June 27, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the receipts for the blocked account is also set for June 27, 2023. Status on close of estate currently set for May 16, 2023 is also continued to June 27, 2023. Any filings are due by June 20, 2023.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Aeyoung Kim. Deputy: G. Tan. Not reported.
